title: AWS S3 Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Amazon S3** is AWS's object-storage primitive. This connector ã `AwsS3Connector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "aws_s3"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) ã wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from S3 (or any S3-compatible provider: Cloudflare R2, Wasabi, Backblaze B2, MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces).

| Surface | Operation | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Buckets | `ListBuckets` / `CreateBucket` / `DeleteBucket` / `HeadBucket` | Account-level bucket inventory + CRUD |
| Objects (read) | `ListObjectsV2` / `HeadObject` / `GetObject` | List, head, fetch object bodies |
| Objects (write) | `PutObject` / `CopyObject` / `DeleteObject` / `PutObjectAcl` | Upload, server-side copy, delete, ACL |
| Versions | `ListObjectVersions` | Versioned-bucket inventory |
| Presign | `generate_presigned_url` | Time-limited GET/PUT URLs ã no server bytes transit |

The connector normalises S3 objects + buckets into `NormalizedDocument` (`id = f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`) via `helpers/normalizer.py`. All public methods are standalone `async def` on `AwsS3Connector` (OCP ã new ops add a method without modifying the base). HTTP / SigV4 / retry orchestration lives in `client/http_client.py::S3HTTPClient` (SOC). We use **aiobotocore** end-to-end so the event loop never blocks and SigV4 is delegated to botocore ã never hand-rolled.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Issue AWS credentials

1. Open the **AWS IAM Console** ã **Users** ã choose / create a programmatic user.
2. **Security credentials** ã **Create access key** ã choose **Application running outside AWS**.
3. Copy the **Access key ID** (`AKIAãÎ`) and the **Secret access key** (shown ONCE).
4. Attach (or inline) an IAM policy that grants the minimum S3 actions you need ã see the **Configuration** section below for a least-privilege template.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ã Manage Connectors ã AWS S3**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `access_key_id` (string) | `AKIAãÎ` from step 3 |
| `secret_access_key` (secret) | the matching secret |
| `region` (string) | e.g. `us-east-1`, `eu-west-1`, `ap-south-1` |
| `endpoint_url` (string, optional) | only for non-AWS providers (R2 / Wasabi / Backblaze / MinIO) |
| `session_token` (secret, optional) | required ONLY for STS / federated identity |
| `default_bucket` (string, optional) | UI convenience + drives health-probe scope |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `access_key_id` + `secret_access_key` are present (it does NOT call AWS), returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which calls `ListBuckets` (or `HeadBucket` on `default_bucket` if configured). On 2xx ã `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On `AccessDenied`/`InvalidAccessKeyId` ã `DEGRADED + EXPIRED`. On `EndpointConnectionError` ã `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with the network message. On `NoSuchBucket` (default bucket only) ã `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` ã credentials are fine, bucket name needs fixing.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
AWS S3 uses **Signature V4** request signing ã every request is signed with the caller's access key + secret. There is no token exchange and no refresh cycle for long-lived IAM access keys (STS session tokens DO expire ã see below).

### Credential model

| Field | Sent as | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `access_key_id` | SigV4 credential scope | Required. 20-char `AKIAãÎ` |
| `secret_access_key` | SigV4 signing key derivation | Required. 40-char base64-like string |
| `session_token` | `X-Amz-Security-Token` header | Optional. STS / AssumeRole credentials |
| `region` | SigV4 credential scope + endpoint construction | Default `us-east-1` |
| `endpoint_url` | base URL override | Optional. R2 / Wasabi / Backblaze / MinIO |

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `access_key_id` + `secret_access_key`. Saves merged config. Does **not** call AWS. |
| `authorize(...)` | Not implemented ã there is no OAuth flow. |
| `health_check()` | `ListBuckets` (default) OR `HeadBucket(default_bucket)` when set. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called ã no token to refresh (STS rotation is operator-driven). |
| token storage | None ã `secret_access_key` lives in `self.config`, encrypted at rest by the gateway. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request is signed by `aiobotocore` and carries the SigV4 canonical headers:

```
Authorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=<key>/<date>/<region>/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=ãÎ, Signature=ãÎ
x-amz-date: <YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ>
x-amz-content-sha256: <body-sha256>
x-amz-security-token: <session_token>      ã only when STS
```

The connector layer never touches these ã it would be a footgun to roll our own SigV4.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ã never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `access_key_id` | string | yes | `self.access_key_id` | IAM access key id (`AKIAãÎ`) |
| `secret_access_key` | secret | yes | `self.secret_access_key` | Paired secret (40 chars) |
| `region` | string | yes (default `us-east-1`) | `self.region` | AWS region code |
| `endpoint_url` | string | no | `self.endpoint_url` | Override for R2 / Wasabi / Backblaze / MinIO |
| `session_token` | secret | no | `self.session_token` | STS / federated-identity token |
| `default_bucket` | string | no | `self.default_bucket` | UI pre-fill + health-probe scope |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["access_key_id", "secret_access_key", "region"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED", "EXPIRED"),
    403: ("DEGRADED", "EXPIRED"),
    429: ("DEGRADED", "CONNECTED"),
}
```

### Minimum-privilege IAM policy

For a tenant that lists + reads + writes one bucket and nothing else:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "BucketLevel",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": ["s3:ListBucket", "s3:GetBucketLocation"],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::TENANT-BUCKET"
    },
    {
      "Sid": "ObjectLevel",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": ["s3:GetObject", "s3:PutObject", "s3:DeleteObject", "s3:GetObjectAcl", "s3:PutObjectAcl"],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::TENANT-BUCKET/*"
    }
  ]
}
```

If `default_bucket` is NOT configured, `health_check()` calls `ListBuckets`, which requires `s3:ListAllMyBuckets` at the account level. Either grant that action OR set `default_bucket` so the probe uses `HeadBucket` instead.

### S3-compatible providers

| Provider | `endpoint_url` | `region` | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cloudflare R2 | `https://<account_id>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com` | `auto` | Uses R2 API tokens cast as IAM credentials |
| Wasabi | `https://s3.<region>.wasabisys.com` | regional (e.g. `us-east-1`) | Drop-in S3 API |
| Backblaze B2 | `https://s3.<region>.backblazeb2.com` | regional | S3-compatible mode required on the bucket |
| MinIO | `https://minio.example.com` | `us-east-1` | Self-hosted; ensure TLS cert is trusted |
| DigitalOcean Spaces | `https://<region>.digitaloceanspaces.com` | regional | API-key / secret pair from DO console |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `AwsS3Connector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP + SigV4 live in `client/http_client.py::S3HTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `access_key_id` + `secret_access_key`. Save merged config. Does NOT call AWS.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Calls `ListBuckets` (or `HeadBucket(default_bucket)` when set). Classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
No-op for S3 ã returns `SyncResult(COMPLETED, 0/0/0)` with explanatory message. Ingestion is per-call: `list_objects` + `get_object` + caller-owned ingest.

children.title: Buckets
children.content:
### `async list_buckets() -> List[Dict]`
Returns `[{name, creation_date(ISO-8601 UTC)}]`.

### `async create_bucket(bucket, region=None) -> Dict`
Honours the us-east-1 LocationConstraint quirk (omitted for us-east-1).

### `async delete_bucket(bucket) -> Dict`
Bucket MUST be empty (AWS enforces server-side).

### `async head_bucket(bucket) -> Dict`
Existence + access probe ã `{bucket, exists: True}` on 2xx, raises `AwsS3NotFound` on 404.

children.title: Objects
children.content:
### `async list_objects(bucket, prefix="", max_keys=1000, continuation_token=None) -> Dict`
`ListObjectsV2` with token pagination. Returns `{bucket, prefix, objects, is_truncated, next_continuation_token, key_count}`.

### `async list_object_versions(bucket, prefix="", max_keys=1000) -> Dict`
Versioned-bucket inventory. Returns `{versions, delete_markers, is_truncated}`.

### `async get_object_metadata(bucket, key) -> Dict`
`HeadObject` ã `{bucket, key, size, etag, content_type, last_modified, metadata, version_id}`.

### `async get_object(bucket, key) -> bytes`
`GetObject` body drained into memory. For very large objects, prefer `generate_presigned_url("get_object")`.

### `async put_object(bucket, key, body, content_type, metadata=None) -> Dict`
`PutObject` with user metadata sanitisation (ASCII strings only).

### `async copy_object(source_bucket, source_key, dest_bucket, dest_key) -> Dict`
Server-side copy. No body transit through the connector.

### `async delete_object(bucket, key) -> Dict`
Idempotent on AWS ã succeeds even when the key is missing.

### `async set_object_acl(bucket, key, acl) -> Dict`
`PutObjectAcl`. `acl ã {"private", "public-read", "public-read-write", "authenticated-read", "bucket-owner-read", "bucket-owner-full-control"}`.

children.title: Presigned URLs
children.content:
### `async generate_presigned_url(bucket, key, operation="get_object", expires_in=3600) -> Dict`
Local SigV4 signing ã no network round-trip. `operation` is the boto3 method name (`get_object` for downloads, `put_object` for uploads). AWS caps `expires_in` to 604800 (7 days) for SigV4 GET URLs.

Returned shape: `{url, bucket, key, operation, expires_in}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
AwsS3Error                # base; carries status_code + response_body
ããã AwsS3AuthError        # 401 / 403 ã AccessDenied, InvalidAccessKeyId, SignatureDoesNotMatch, ExpiredToken
ããã AwsS3NotFound         # 404 ã NoSuchKey, NoSuchBucket
ããã AwsS3NetworkError     # transport ã DNS, socket, TLS, EndpointConnectionError, 5xx
```

### Classification

`helpers/utils.classify_client_error` maps `botocore.exceptions.ClientError` and `EndpointConnectionError` to typed exceptions based on `Error.Code` and HTTP status ã independent of which `aiobotocore` operation raised them.

| AWS Error Code | HTTP | Typed exception |
|---|---|---|
| `NoSuchKey`, `NoSuchBucket`, `NotFound` | 404 | `AwsS3NotFound` |
| `AccessDenied`, `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`, `ExpiredToken`, `InvalidToken`, `TokenRefreshRequired` | 401/403 | `AwsS3AuthError` |
| (anything else with a Code) | other | `AwsS3Error` (status_code populated) |
| `EndpointConnectionError`, `BotoCoreError` | transport | `AwsS3NetworkError` |

### Retry behaviour (`helpers/utils.with_retry`)

| Class | Action |
|---|---|
| `AwsS3AuthError` / `AwsS3NotFound` | Raise immediately ã no retry |
| `AwsS3NetworkError` | Exponential backoff with jitter, max 3 attempts |
| `AwsS3Error` with `status_code` 0 or 5xx | Same backoff |
| `AwsS3Error` with `status_code` in {400, 409, ãÎ} | Raise immediately |

Backoff sequence: `RETRY_DELAY_S * BACKOFF_FACTOR ** attempt + uniform(0, 0.5)`, capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S=32s`.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps them:

```
AccessDenied / InvalidAccessKeyId ã ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, EXPIRED)
NoSuchBucket on default_bucket    ã ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)   (bucket name wrong, creds OK)
EndpointConnectionError            ã ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)   (network issue)
other                              ã ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED) with raw message
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `access_key_id` or `secret_access_key` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. AWS shows the secret only ONCE at creation ã rotate the key if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, EXPIRED)` at health_check, code = `InvalidAccessKeyId` | Access key revoked, deactivated, or typo'd | In AWS IAM Console ã Users ã Security credentials, confirm the key is Active. Rotate if needed. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, EXPIRED)` at health_check, code = `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | Secret access key has a leading/trailing space or wrong character | Re-paste the secret. Common when copying from a CSV file with a trailing newline. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, EXPIRED)` at health_check, code = `AccessDenied` | IAM policy doesn't grant `s3:ListAllMyBuckets` | Either grant `s3:ListAllMyBuckets` OR configure `default_bucket` so the probe uses `HeadBucket` instead. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, EXPIRED)`, code = `ExpiredToken` | STS session token has expired (15 min ã 12 h lifetime) | Refresh the session via AssumeRole and update `session_token`. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)` with `NoSuchBucket` | `default_bucket` is wrong or the bucket was deleted | Fix or remove `default_bucket` in ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)` with `EndpointConnectionError` | DNS failure, VPC egress blocked, wrong `endpoint_url` for R2/Wasabi/etc. | Verify outbound HTTPS to the S3 endpoint. For non-AWS providers, double-check `endpoint_url` matches the provider docs exactly. |
| `BucketNotEmpty` on `delete_bucket` | The bucket still has objects | Loop `list_objects` + `delete_object` to drain it first. |
| `PermanentRedirect` on `put_object` | Bucket lives in a different region than the configured `region` | Set `region` to the bucket's actual region; SigV4 uses region in the credential scope. |
| `aws_s3.transient_error` warnings spike | Hitting AWS / provider rate cap | The client retries with exponential backoff. Reduce concurrent calls or batch differently. |
| `EntityTooLarge` on `put_object` | Single-PUT > 5 GB (AWS hard limit) | Use a multi-part upload ã outside this connector's surface today. |
| 100% of calls fail after a credential rotation | Old credentials cached upstream of the connector | Restart the gateway pod, or rotate `connector_id` to force a fresh `S3HTTPClient`. |
